items.headline: Major commercial project approved on West San Carlos
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, one of San José's key commercial corridors. This represents significant new business or industrial activity in the area.

items.headline: City commissions set priorities for next fiscal year
items.summary: Saratoga's five commissions—covering heritage preservation, library/public art, parks, traffic safety, and planning—presented their work plans for FY 2026-27. The plans reveal what city priorities will get focused attention, including traffic safety initiatives and park/recreation projects.

items.headline: Stadium neighborhood relations committee continues addressing Levi's Stadium issues
items.summary: A subcommittee focused on Stadium neighborhood relations is meeting to handle ongoing concerns from residents near Levi's Stadium. Residents can submit comments about stadium-related impacts on their neighborhood.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city council approved an amnesty program to help businesses get compliant with licensing requirements, paired with outreach to shopping centers. This could affect local retailers and small business owners who may have fallen behind on permits or licenses.

items.headline: City announces economic development incentives for retail and startups
items.summary: Cupertino is introducing new incentive programs aimed at attracting and supporting retail businesses and startups. Residents may see this reflected in new shops, services, or job opportunities in the community.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up becomes annual city event
items.summary: City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an official annual City-sponsored special event with city funding and permission to accept material donations. Residents can now expect this community cleanup event to recur each year.

items.headline: City seeks public input on housing grant funding priorities
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission heard presentations from applicants seeking Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25. This federal funding supports affordable housing and community development projects, making it worth monitoring if you care about housing affordability or local development.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study San Antonio Road area transportation and land use
items.summary: The commission will examine alternatives for how the San Antonio Road corridor should develop, including transportation options and zoning changes. This study could affect future development and traffic patterns in that area of Palo Alto.
